TITLE: chore: Dev-tooling — .claude settings allowlist, senior-reviewer refresh, verify/run skill
LABELS: roadmap, type:chore, size:S, phase:A-foundation
---
## Context

Three small dev-experience gaps slow down every Claude Code session on this repo: (1) the mandatory senior-reviewer quality-gate agent still believes the project has no automated tests, so reviews under-weight the actual 94-test suite and ask for "manual testing instructions" instead of demanding pytest runs; (2) there is no `.claude/settings.json`, so every read-only `git`/`pytest` command triggers a permission prompt; (3) there is no documented, skill-encoded way to launch the app headlessly (`QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen`), which agents rediscover by trial and error each session. All three are one-file fixes.

## Current state

- `.claude/agents/senior-reviewer.md` (77 lines) — the review-dimension list contains a stale claim at **line 28**:

  ```
  3. **Tests.** This project has no automated tests (yet). For any new feature, flag whether manual testing instructions are at least present in the commit message or a plan file. If a feature could regress silently, that's P1 minimum.
  ```

  Reality: `tests/` holds unit, integration, and ui suites (94 passing tests, pytest + pytest-qt, see `CLAUDE.md` Tech Stack), CI runs them on 3 OS × Python 3.10–3.14 (`.github/workflows/tests.yml`), and `tests/integration/test_smoke.py` includes the AST inline-import gate (ADR-016). The rest of the agent file (persona, P0/P1/P2 discipline, CLAUDE.md-compliance checklist at lines 33–38) is current and must not be reworded.
- `.claude/settings.json` — does not exist (`.claude/` contains only `agents/` and `skills/`).
- `.claude/skills/` — contains only `temp-roadmap/` (its `SKILL.md` shows the house frontmatter format: `name` + `description` YAML block, then markdown body).
- Headless-launch knowledge currently lives only implicitly: `tests/conftest.py:13` sets `QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen`, and CI (`.github/workflows/tests.yml:26–37`) documents the Linux Qt6 runtime packages (`libxcb-cursor0`, `libegl1`, `libgl1`, XCB plugin set, xvfb).
- App entry points: `python -m src.digitalsreeni_image_annotator.main`, `digitalsreeni-image-annotator`, `sreeni` (CLAUDE.md Quick Reference); smoke tests: `pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`.

## Proposed design

Three additions, one file each:

**1. Fix `.claude/agents/senior-reviewer.md` line 28.** Replace the quoted line with:

```
3. **Tests.** This project has a real pytest + pytest-qt suite (`tests/unit`, `tests/integration`, `tests/ui`; run `pytest tests/ -v`, headless via `QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen`). For any behavioural change, verify tests were added or updated and actually run — a diff that touches controllers/widgets/io with no test delta needs an explicit justification. Missing tests for a silently-regressable path is P1 minimum; a broken or skipped existing test is P0. The smoke suite (`pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`, incl. the ADR-016 AST inline-import gate) must pass before any PR.
```

**2. Add `.claude/settings.json`** (project-shared, checked in) with a read-only + test-run allowlist:

```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Bash(git status:*)",
      "Bash(git log:*)",
      "Bash(git diff:*)",
      "Bash(git show:*)",
      "Bash(git branch:*)",
      "Bash(git grep:*)",
      "Bash(git remote:*)",
      "Bash(ls:*)",
      "Bash(pytest:*)",
      "Bash(python -m pytest:*)",
      "Bash(python --version:*)",
      "Bash(python -c:*)",
      "Bash(pip list:*)",
      "Bash(pip show:*)"
    ]
  }
}
```

Notes: no `git push`/`commit`/`checkout`, no `pip install`, no `rm` — anything mutating still prompts. `pytest` is included deliberately: it only writes coverage artifacts (`htmlcov/`, `coverage.xml`) per `pytest.ini` addopts.

**3. Add `.claude/skills/run-app/SKILL.md`** documenting headless launch + smoke verification:

```markdown
---
name: run-app
description: Launch the DigitalSreeni Image Annotator headlessly and run its smoke tests. Use when asked to run/launch the app, verify it starts after a change, or check for import/startup errors in a display-less (web/CI) session.
---

# Run the app headlessly + smoke tests

This is a PyQt6 GUI app; sessions without a display must use Qt's offscreen
platform plugin. GUI interaction is impossible offscreen — a clean launch
proves imports, window construction, and signal wiring, nothing more.

## Launch check (no display)

    QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen timeout 10 python -m src.digitalsreeni_image_annotator.main

- Exit code 124 (timeout killed it) = SUCCESS: the app started and stayed up.
- Any traceback before the timeout = startup failure; fix before proceeding.
- Installed entry points work the same way: `timeout 10 digitalsreeni-image-annotator` / `sreeni`.

## Smoke tests (always run after code changes)

    pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v

Includes the AST-based inline-import gate (ADR-016) — a "clean-looking"
launch alone is NOT sufficient after moving modules. Full suite: `pytest tests/ -v`.

## Linux runtime deps

If Qt fails to load its platform plugin, install the Qt6 runtime set
(Debian/Ubuntu): libxcb-cursor0 libegl1 libgl1 libxcb-xinerama0
libxkbcommon-x11-0 (see .github/workflows/tests.yml for the full CI list).

## Limits

Do not claim GUI behaviour (rendering, dark mode, mouse interaction) was
"verified" from an offscreen launch — say so explicitly and fall back to
the test suite or ask the user to check visually.
```

## Implementation steps

1. Create branch `feature/dev-tooling-refresh`.
2. Edit `.claude/agents/senior-reviewer.md`: replace line 28 exactly as specified in Proposed design. Change nothing else in the file.
3. Create `.claude/settings.json` with the JSON above; validate with `python -m json.tool .claude/settings.json`.
4. Create `.claude/skills/run-app/SKILL.md` with the content above (match `temp-roadmap/SKILL.md`'s frontmatter style — `name` and `description` keys only).
5. Sanity-run the skill's own instructions once: the headless launch command (expect exit 124) and the smoke tests (expect pass). If the launch command fails in your environment for a dependency reason, fix the skill text to match reality — the skill must never document a command that doesn't work in this repo.
6. Grep for other stale "no tests" claims in dev tooling: `grep -rni "no automated tests\|manual testing only" .claude/ CLAUDE.md` — fix any additional hits inside `.claude/` (do not touch `docs/09` ADR-004 here; that belongs to the docs-currency issue).
7. Optional (only if trivially verifiable in-session): add a `SessionStart` hook to `.claude/settings.json` that installs dev deps for web sessions. If you cannot verify hook execution, skip it and note the omission in the PR description rather than shipping an untested hook.
8. Run smoke tests; commit.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `.claude/agents/senior-reviewer.md` no longer contains the string "no automated tests"; its Tests dimension instructs running the pytest suite and grades missing/broken tests as P1/P0.
- [ ] No other line of `senior-reviewer.md` changed (diff touches only the Tests dimension).
- [ ] `.claude/settings.json` exists, parses (`python -m json.tool` exits 0), and allowlists only read-only commands plus pytest — no push/commit/install/rm entries.
- [ ] `.claude/skills/run-app/SKILL.md` exists with valid frontmatter (`name: run-app`, non-empty `description`) and documents: offscreen launch command with exit-code interpretation, smoke-test command, Linux Qt deps pointer, and the "offscreen ≠ GUI-verified" caveat.
- [ ] The documented launch command actually works in this repo: `QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen timeout 10 python -m src.digitalsreeni_image_annotator.main` exits 124 with no traceback.
- [ ] `grep -rni "no automated tests" .claude/` returns nothing.
- [ ] `pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v` passes.

## Test plan

No new pytest files (config/docs-only change). Verification commands:

```bash
python -m json.tool .claude/settings.json
QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen timeout 10 python -m src.digitalsreeni_image_annotator.main; echo "exit=$?"   # expect exit=124
pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v
grep -rni "no automated tests" .claude/    # expect no output
```

Plus a live check: start a fresh Claude Code session (or ask the reviewer to) and confirm `git status` / `pytest` no longer prompt.

## Docs to update

- `CLAUDE.md` — add one line under "Development Workflow" or "Quick Tips" pointing at the `run-app` skill for headless launches (keep it to a single line; CLAUDE.md is already dense).
- No arc42 changes: `.claude/` tooling is not application architecture. Do **not** update ADR-004 here — that edit is owned by the docs-currency issue.

## Pitfalls & guardrails

- The senior-reviewer agent is the repo's **mandatory quality gate** (CLAUDE.md: "Run it in the foreground — never `run_in_background: true`"). Keep the persona, severity discipline, and the CLAUDE.md-compliance checklist (lines 33–38, incl. the ADR-013 re-entrancy check) byte-identical — this issue fixes one factual claim, it does not restyle the agent.
- Permission allowlist scope: resist adding convenience entries like `Bash(git checkout:*)` or `Bash(pip install:*)` — the point of the file is *safe* friction removal; mutating commands must keep prompting.
- Skill descriptions drive automatic triggering: the `description` frontmatter must state *when to use it* (run/launch/verify-startup requests), mirroring `temp-roadmap/SKILL.md`'s style.
- A launch that "looks clean" is NOT sufficient after module moves — inline imports fail only when the function is called at runtime (ADR-016); the skill text must keep pointing at the smoke suite, and step 5 requires actually executing both commands before shipping them.
- If adding the optional SessionStart hook, it must be idempotent and fast (guard on a marker file); an untested hook that breaks session startup is worse than no hook.

## Dependencies

None.

_Workflow: follow CLAUDE.md — feature branch, run smoke tests (`pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`), update arc42 docs, run the senior-reviewer agent before the PR._
